---
title: "Projects"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    css: styles.css
---

<div class="section-title">Graduate Research Projects</div>

The projects below represent graduate-level research conducted at Emory University,
combining statistical modeling, machine learning, and real-world data to address
problems in biomedical research and financial analysis.

---

<div class="project-card">
  <h3>Deep Learning for Alzheimer's Disease Neuropathology Trajectory Prediction</h3>
  <div class="project-tags">
    <span class="tag">Deep Learning</span>
    <span class="tag">LSTM</span>
    <span class="tag">Longitudinal Data</span>
    <span class="tag">R</span>
    <span class="tag">Python</span>
  </div>
  <p>
    Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative condition whose
    underlying pathological changes — including amyloid plaque accumulation and
    neurofibrillary tau tangles — begin years before clinical symptoms appear.
    Early and accurate prediction of neuropathological trajectories could help
    identify at-risk individuals and support clinical decision-making.
  </p>
  <p>
    In this project, I developed and evaluated Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and
    Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) models to predict longitudinal neuropathology scores
    using data from the Religious Orders Study and Memory and Aging Project (ROS/MAP),
    a large ongoing cohort of aging adults. The models were trained on multivariate
    time-series inputs including cognitive assessments, demographic factors, and
    biomarker data. Model performance was evaluated using mean absolute error and
    correlation metrics, with attention to interpretability of temporal patterns
    learned by the network.
  </p>
  <p>
    This work was conducted as part of my Research Assistantship under Professor
    Jingjing Yang at Emory University's Department of Biostatistics and Bioinformatics.
  </p>
  <a href="https://github.com/RichardFranklinBAO/AD_Pathology_Trajectory_DL"
     target="_blank" class="project-link">
    View on GitHub
  </a>
</div>

<div class="project-card">
  <h3>S&P 500 Stock Data Interactive Dashboard (2013–2018)</h3>
  <div class="project-tags">
    <span class="tag">Data Visualization</span>
    <span class="tag">R</span>
    <span class="tag">Plotly</span>
    <span class="tag">flexdashboard</span>
    <span class="tag">Financial Data</span>
  </div>
  <p>
    Understanding individual stock behavior within a broad market index requires
    tools that go beyond summary statistics. This project produced an interactive
    web dashboard that allows users to explore five years of daily closing price
    data for all 505 S&P 500 constituent stocks, without requiring any programming
    knowledge.
  </p>
  <p>
    The dashboard was built using R, flexdashboard, Plotly, and the DT package.
    It features two interactive widgets: a dynamic line chart where users can select
    any ticker from a dropdown menu and inspect closing price trends with hover
    tooltips showing date, price, and trading volume; and a sortable, filterable
    performance summary table covering metrics including total return, annualized
    volatility, maximum and minimum closing prices, and average daily trading volume
    across the full five-year window. Color-coded conditional formatting highlights
    high-return and high-volatility stocks at a glance.
  </p>
  <p>
    The dashboard is publicly deployed on GitHub Pages and the source code is
    fully reproducible.
  </p>
  <a href="https://github.com/RichardFranklinBAO/data555_sp500_dashboard"
     target="_blank" class="project-link" style="margin-right: 10px;">
    View on GitHub
  </a>
  <a href="https://richardfranklinbao.github.io/data555_sp500_dashboard/Final_Project_3a_flex.html"
     target="_blank" class="project-link">
    Live Dashboard
  </a>
</div>

<div class="site-footer">
  © 2026 Conglin (Franklin) Bao &nbsp;·&nbsp;
  Built with R Markdown &nbsp;·&nbsp;
  <a href="https://github.com/RichardFranklinBAO" target="_blank">GitHub</a>
</div>
